svg.text: Angular 21 SPA · .NET 10 BFF · Microsoft Entra ID · OIDC Code+PKCE · Cookie session

g.text: [cookie] = HttpOnly · Secure · SameSite=Strict · PKCE = code-verifier+challenge · SLO = single sign-out
Scope: UI + BFF only · API treated as black box returning DTOs · Demo routes (__demo/*) filtered out
SYSTEM CONTEXT — bird's-eye, who calls whom, where the trust boundary is
HttpOnly · Secure · SameSite=Strict · 8h sliding · encrypted ticket
readable by JS · echoed in X-XSRF-TOKEN header (double-submit)
TRUSTED ZONE — Web.UI BFF host (.NET 10, Kestrel)
OnValidatePrincipal hook · refresh in last 5 min
→ never mix Api scope + Graph scope
"Black box" for this deck — separate session covers
Falls back to claim-only shape on failure
DB-first: schema-as-source-of-truth via DbMigrator + customised EF scaffold templates + DtoGen
BFF has no DB dependency — all persistence in API tier
Serilog 4 + OpenTelemetry · vendor-neutral wire format · degrades silently if unreachable (logs buffer)
END-TO-END SEQUENCE — cold load → first-time auth → authenticated request → token refresh → logout
authGuard on '/dashboard' sees no session → createUrlTree(['/auth/login'], { queryParams: { returnUrl } })
STAGE 2 — User clicks Sign in · OIDC code+PKCE flow
Top-level navigation, NOT XHR — cookie writes survive cross-origin redirects only this way
redirect chain → user lands on Entra login page
302 form_post Location: https://app/signin-oidc + auto-submit form { code, state }
POST /signin-oidc { code, state } — body, not URL (form_post)
STAGE 3 — Authenticated bootstrap · session probe + antiforgery seed
200 { isAuthenticated: true, name, email, roles, expiresAt }
AuthStore.hydrate() → /api/auth/me/permissions (placeholder until D4) → roles + bypass + ttl
Router.navigate('/dashboard') · authGuard passes · permissionGuard checks AuthStore.hasAllPermissions(...)
STAGE 4 — Authenticated proxy hop · 8 interceptors → cookie auth → XSRF check → Bearer attach → API
SPA-side interceptor chain (clone request, set headers): correlation → security → cache → dedup → loading → logging → retry → error
read tokens from ticket · skip refresh (>5 min remaining)
[Authorize] satisfied · [AutoValidateAntiforgeryToken] compares cookie vs header · GetTokenAsync('access_token')
forward to ApiBaseUri/v1/users · Authorization: Bearer eyJ... · hop-by-hop headers stripped (RFC 7230 §6.1)
200 [DTOs] · response interceptors: cache populate · dedup release · loading.dec · log("http.response 200 in 47ms")
STAGE 5 — Token refresh (last 5 min of access-token life)
expires in < 5 min → ValidateAsync calls Entra
POST /api/auth/logout (form, not XHR) → SignOut(props, CookieScheme, OidcScheme)
BroadcastChannel('ep:auth') sends 'logout' message → other tabs navigate to /auth/login
BFF INTERNALS — Web.UI host folder map · pipeline · named HTTP clients · CSP · health · rate limiter
Convention: 1 line in Program.cs per concern → AddPlatformX() in Setup/PlatformX.cs
PlatformAntiforgerySetup — XSRF cookie + X-XSRF-TOKEN header (env-aware SecurePolicy)
PlatformRateLimiterSetup — per-session 120/m, per-IP 600/m, OIDC callbacks exempt
SecurityHeadersMiddleware — per-request CSP nonce (16 bytes) + HSTS + X-Frame + Permissions-Policy
CorrelationIdMiddleware — X-Correlation-ID + LogContext.PushProperty for Serilog scope
SpaFallbackEndpoint — MapFallback("/{**catchAll}", ...) — defense vs nonfile constraint footgun
Authentication/TokenRefreshService — OnValidatePrincipal hook (refresh in last 5 min)
Graph/GraphUserProfileService — /me + photo · IMemoryCache 5min · refresh-token grant for Graph
SessionMetrics — OTel Meter · 4 instruments (Created · Refreshed · RefreshFailed · LifetimeSeconds)
Angular SPA — see Panel 04 for SPA folder map
→ UseStaticFiles (SPA bundle) → UseRouting → UseCors(ep-web-ui) → UseRateLimiter → UseAuthentication → UseAuthorization
→ MapHealthEndpoints (anonymous) → MapControllers → MapSpaFallback (LAST — only catches what nothing else claimed)
Why RateLimiter BEFORE Auth: 429 abusers without an Entra round-trip · Why SpaFallback LAST: every other handler had its shot
Why "/{**catchAll}" not single-arg MapFallback: nonfile constraint excludes file-extensioned paths silently
Why AddControllersWithViews (not AddControllers): [AutoValidateAntiforgeryToken] is in Mvc.ViewFeatures
used by ProxyController.Forward → GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE on ApiBaseUri
refresh-token grant for User.Read (Graph) — separate from API access token (one resource per token)
connect-src 'self' (+ ws://localhost:* in dev for hot reload);
Defense vs single host with many sessions
PartitionedRateLimiter.CreateChained — either bucket exhausting returns 429 + Retry-After header
Action on fail: stop sending traffic
Memorialized footgun — SPA routes like /users/edit.preview would break
SPA STRUCTURE — folder rules · import direction · signal stores · routing · 8 interceptors · chrome
5 top-level folders · one-way import rule (enforced by dependency-cruiser)
/config.json fetcher · I1 initializer · falls back to environment.ts
PrimeUix theme + ripple/zIndex defaults · cssLayer order: theme,base,primeng,utilities
8 functional: correlation → security → cache → dedup → loading → logging → retry → error
cache-invalidation-bus.service — global bus so feature stores can react to cross-feature mutations
BaseApiService — basePath + ttl/skipCache helpers · keeps feature *.api.ts files terse
Logger (PII-scrub) · Notification · LoadingService · CorrelationContextService · FocusManagement · Csp · DomainStore · NavbarConfigService · StatusBannerService
RouteMetadata · CurrentUser · ApiError · EffectivePermissions · feature DTO interfaces
No imports of features/* OR layouts/* — core is the foundation
Sibling features cannot import each other — extract shared to core/ or shared/
UsersStore · OrdersStore · etc — feature-scoped via routes file (lifecycle = nav in/out)
layouts/ — Chrome shells (one per route subtree)
Two 404s: one inside shell (chrome stays), one top-level (no chrome)
shared/ — Reusable, generic, no domain knowledge
data-table · cell-renderer · column-chooser · column-filter · live-data-table — universal kit
page-header · status-badge · status-banner · empty-state · error-state · global-progress-bar · skeleton-card · session-expiring-dialog
platform-navbar · platform-footer · sub-nav-orchestrator · domain factories · NAVBAR_CONFIG_PROVIDER (Static + Backend)
Cannot import from features/, layouts/, core/ — pure presentation
App initializer chain: I1 loadRuntimeConfig → I2 AuthService.refreshSession (BLOCKS bootstrap) → I3 CspViolationReporter → I4 FocusManagement
AppComponent: spinner gate while isLoading() · <router-outlet/> renders when bootstrap resolves
app.routes.ts: 3 subtrees (auth, app-shell, error) + 2 catch-all 404 (one shell-level, one top-level)
Provide ZonelessChangeDetection · provideRouter(routes, withPreloading(CustomPreloader), withViewTransitions, withInMemoryScrolling)
Cardinal rule: interceptors own toasts; stores own inline errors (capture into store.error signal). No double-toasting.
Chain rationale: stamp correlation FIRST → security headers BEFORE cache key → cache hits short-circuit dedup/loading/logging/retry/error
→ loading wraps retries (so spinner shows for full duration) → logging duration matches user-perceived → retry above error (success retry never reaches errorInterceptor)
→ error normalizes any failure shape (HttpErrorResponse, ProblemDetails, legacy {message}) into ApiError {message,statusCode,code?,errors?,correlationId?,timestamp}
XSRF doubly wired: Angular's withXsrfConfiguration (mutating same-origin) + securityInterceptor (every /api/*) — belt and braces
All opt-out headers (X-Cache-TTL, X-Skip-Cache, X-Skip-Dedup, X-Skip-Loading, X-Skip-Retry, X-Skip-Error-Handling) are STRIPPED before forwarding — servers never see them
title · pageHeader · preload:true · showInNav:true · icon pi-home
404 inside shell — keeps chrome visible · pageHeader for breathing room
RouteMetadata in data: { label, icon, breadcrumb, showInNav, requiredPermissions, featureFlag, preload, pageHeader }
→ Five consumers from one declaration: navbar, breadcrumb, page header, preloader, document title
Guards stack as AND: canActivate: [authGuard, permissionGuard('users:read'), anyPermissionGuard('a','b')]
Custom preloader: only data.preload===true AND navigator.connection.saveData!==true → background load
Reachable mid-flow even when auth is broken — minimal chrome
NotFoundComponent · drops chrome (URL didn't match any subtree at all)
1. GlobalProgressBarComponent — top edge, binds to LoadingService.counter
RBAC defense in depth: server authoritative, NavMenu re-filters every render, hides disallowed items
Tone-aware via [data-tone] CSS — same component swaps to palmetto for Healthcare, indigo for Finance
Reads route.snapshot.data.pageHeader → renders title, subtitle, icon, primaryAction button
Single (action) emitter → Shell.onPageHeaderAction → onNavAction dispatcher (one switch, one place)
role=main, id=main-content, tabindex=-1 (focus target after navigation), overflow-x:clip
NavbarConfigService.footer() — palmetto top border (brand) — links: brand, product, resources, legal
<p-toast position="top-right" preventOpenDuplicates> — driven by NotificationService → MessageService
@defer SessionExpiringDialog — lazy, only when session.expiringSoon() signal fires
Status banners (5 severities, ARIA polite/assertive) hosted by StatusBannerHostComponent — slots between sub-nav and main
Adding a new domain: new factory in shared/layout/domains/ + register in DOMAIN_CHROME_REGISTRY (no shell/navbar/footer code changes)
TOKEN REFRESH + LOGOUT — OnValidatePrincipal hook · failure modes · single sign-out
RefreshThreshold = 5 min — generous so Entra round-trip + proxy hop + slow API don't expire mid-flight
7. Entra terminates session (no account picker thanks to id_token_hint) → 302 /signout-callback-oidc
8. OidcScheme handles callback (no-op) → 302 to validated returnUrl (default /)
9. SPA loads / anonymous → /api/auth/session returns Anonymous → router redirects to /auth/login
10. BroadcastChannel('ep:auth') sends 'logout' → other tabs in same browser navigate to /auth/login
Cookie ticket malformed (key rotation w/o ring)
Cookie key not in pod's ring
Share key ring via Key Vault
ERROR MODEL — every failure → ApiError → toast + nav + inline form errors
Cardinal rule that prevents double-toasting: interceptors own toasts, stores own inline errors via store.error signal
Correlation thread — one ID, one query, one user's session
SPA correlationInterceptor (#1) mints crypto.randomUUID() · pushes onto CorrelationContextService · stamps X-Correlation-ID
BFF CorrelationIdMiddleware reads/echoes header · LogContext.PushProperty("CorrelationId", id) · Serilog scope
ProxyController stamps explicit header on outbound to API · sub claim from User.FindFirst("sub") joins user identity to logs · DB query tags carry id (dev only)
SECURITY DEFENSE LAYERS — five concentric layers; attacker must defeat all
Layer 1 — Edge (LB · WAF · TLS · HSTS · CSP · X-Frame-Options)
Layer 2 — Edge rate limit (per-session 120/m, per-IP 600/m, OIDC exempt)
Layer 3 — Authentication (OIDC code+PKCE, HttpOnly cookie, SameSite=Strict)
• XSS in SPA → cannot read ep.bff.session (HttpOnly) or any token
• CSRF from evil.com → cannot read XSRF-TOKEN (same-origin), so cannot forge X-XSRF-TOKEN
• Token theft via leaked logs → tokens never logged; PII scrubbed by LoggerService
• Compromised BFF host → ClientSecret in Key Vault via managed identity; rotation kills sessions
• Compromised DB → access tokens not in DB (in cookie tickets), so DB compromise yields no tokens
• Open-redirect on login/logout → Url.IsLocalUrl() rejects absolute / protocol-relative URLs
• Browser NEVER sees a Bearer token (verified in auth.service.ts — no token in SessionInfo)
• JS NEVER reads ep.bff.session (HttpOnly enforced in PlatformAuthenticationSetup.cs)
• XSRF-TOKEN IS readable by JS (that's the point — double-submit pattern)
• Refresh tokens NEVER leave the BFF — TokenRefreshService calls Entra server-side via named HTTP client
• ClientSecret NEVER in any file — env var or Key Vault via managed identity
• Tokens NEVER in localStorage / sessionStorage — only in cookie ticket (encrypted, HttpOnly)
TECH STACK — versions and the *why* (from package.json + .csproj as of 2026-04-28)
Required by Angular; we lean on signals
Encrypts cookie ticket; ring shared via Key Vault
BFF has NO DB dependency — stateless across pods (with shared key ring) — all persistence in API tier
TRADEOFFS — load-bearing decisions, with the alternative we considered
Token-out-of-browser; works on Safari ITP; simpler logout
Refresh per-request, no idle work, multi-pod safe
Footprint; explicit auth swap is more readable while foundation forms
Closest to user; one source of truth
Every cached endpoint is a deliberate decision
Belt and braces — built-in covers mutating, custom covers all /api/*
Signals + types + small surface; no reducer boilerplate
Component breadth + token-driven theming; 17:1 in evaluation
New canonical engine; primeng package stays v21
UI Kit demo route covers ground; 554 transitive deps gone
Karma deprecated by Angular; Vitest is ~10× faster cold
Walks lazy chunks; ESLint sees one file at a time
frame-ancestors works only as header; nonce ready for inline scripts
• Polly retry on TokenRefreshService HTTP client (avoid 401 on transient Entra blip)
• OTEL-web initialization in SPA (W3C traceparent ↔ X-Correlation-ID 1:1 alignment)
• Back-channel logout from Entra (multi-region SSO termination)
• PrimeNG CSP nonce mode → drop style-src 'unsafe-inline'
• Self-host fonts → drop fonts.gstatic.com / fonts.googleapis.com from CSP
• MFA step-up on sensitive ops via prompt=login (step-up authentication)
• Session expiry warning dialog (SessionMonitorService UI is the missing piece)
• OpenAPI types codegen for /api/* clients (eliminate hand-typed DTOs)
• Storybook reintroduction (if UI Kit grows past current ~14 categories)
• Per-feature Plop generators (scaffold UsersStore + UsersApi + USERS_ROUTES with one prompt)
• Synthetic monitoring against /api/auth/session (1m heartbeat from external prober)
• Per-route latency histograms (apdex per top-level route)
• SLO dashboards (apdex, error budget) — App Insights workbook
• Bundle-size budget gates wired into CI (npm run bundle:check already exists)
• ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) for every "Picked vs Alternative" — currently in deck only
• Threat-modeling session (STRIDE) per major feature surface
• Disaster-recovery runbook for cookie-key compromise · Entra outage · cache stampede
• Quarterly dependency hygiene review (track in Docs/Implementation/Compliance-TODO.md)
Enterprise Platform · UI + BFF Architecture Megamap
Companion to docs/Architecture/Demo/ (12 markdown docs · ~50 Mermaid diagrams · 0 PNGs)
